regime_reason: US-Iran tensions re-escalated sharply (Trump declared the ceasefire memorandum 'over' after US strikes on Iranian-linked targets), sending oil up over 5% and pressuring equities broadly (Dow -1.1%, S&P -0.3%, Nasdaq pared to near-flat) with VIX up 3.4% to 16.67; only energy and consumer defensive closed green.

watchlist.note: Transocean: >$1B multi-year Equinor harsh-environment rig contract (7/8), Q2 earnings set for 8/5.
Absci: Truist PT raised to $17 (7/2) after positive Phase 1 ABS-201 data (6/24); +47% on the week.
Hudson Technologies: 10%+ owner Hartree Partners bought 764,202 sh ($4.48M) over 6/30-7/2, board also adding.

notes: User watchlist ticker DVLT is a serial diluter -- shares outstanding up ~1518% YoY to 855M after a $60M raise in May, and a newly proposed ~$2B structured financing could dilute over 50% more; today's pop is tied to a speculative minerals-tokenization MOU and a meme-coin launch, not a real re-rating catalyst, so it goes to avoid despite the headline move. Held position VMAR (42 sh @ $1.41, now $1.22/-13.5%, stop $1.30) has no new negative news today beyond the already-flagged reverse split and 7/2 insider sale. New avoid entries: REE (Nasdaq-delisted 7/7, -64% to $0.07) and CASI (delisted 7/6, -39% to $0.15) both crashed post-delisting; EOSE priced a dilutive $75M raise with Hudson Bay Capital and is down ~40% in weeks to $4.73.
